# VALIDATION.md -- trust gate (auto-generated by `openmucf.validate`)

Engine reproduction of the pre-registered targets in `openmucf/data/validation_targets.csv` (see `PRE_REGISTRATION.md`). The `class` column is the claim tier: only `independent` rows are genuine predictions -- and as of v1 the passing set contains none (the three `independent` rows are registered, pre-framed FAILs, below).
Operating point for sticking-controlled checks: **T=300 K, phi=1.2, c_t=0.5** (canonical liquid).

| target | class | observed | predicted | tolerance | verdict | note |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V_kouchen_base | reproduction (fed input) | 112.6 (fed omega_s_eff=0.557%) | 114.5 | +-10% | PASS | collision-only baseline |
| V_kouchen_best | reproduction (fed input) | 156.5 (fed omega_s_eff=0.308%) | 160.3 | +-10% | PASS | best external-field scenario |
| V_petitjean | reproduction (fed input) | 113 (fed omega_s_eff=0.45%) | 130.5 | [100,150] | PASS | measured liquid effective sticking |
| V_yamashita_lcT | shape (calibrated model) | monotone X_mu(T) rise | 125.2 | monotone | PASS | X_mu(200,400,600,800 K)=[111.2, 116.7, 121.2, 125.2]. The three Yamashita rows (lcT monotonicity + ratio/curve vs the digitized full curve) are one test (the same lambda_c(T)/X_mu(T) inversion); counted as a single shape check in the summary. |
| V_breunlich_lambdac | anchor-consistency | 1.45e8 s^-1 (max measured cycling rate, liquid; Breunlich 1989) | 143822639.2 | +-30% | PASS | engine-implied cycling rate at (300 K, 1.2 phi, c_t=0.5), inverted from the closed form; inherits the formation-scale anchor (see gate rule) |
| V_yamashita_ratio | independent | 2.358 (digitized full-curve lambda_c(800 K)/lambda_c(300 K) centreline; Yamashita-Kino Fig.3a, data/yamashita_kino_lc_T.csv) | 1.3 | +-30% | **FAIL** | engine ratio of implied lambda_c at 800 K vs 300 K (same inversion as V_breunlich) vs the digitized Yamashita-Kino full-curve centreline ratio 2.358 (band [2.091, 2.625]; solid-line 2.235). Re-anchored 2026-07-13: the earlier ~1.45 comparator was a digitization under-read, so the corrected, strictly-harder target flips this row PASS->FAIL (engine ~1.31 is -44% vs the centreline / -41% vs the solid line -- outside +-30% across the whole digitization band). Registered expected-FAIL finding: the first sourced quantification of the v1 placeholder's temperature-shape deficit (no input tuned; formation.py untouched). The three Yamashita rows (lcT, ratio, curve) are one test (the same lambda_c(T)/X_mu(T) inversion), counted once below. |
| V_yamashita_curve | independent | digitized lambda_c(T)/lambda_c(300) at 200/400/600/800 K (Yamashita-Kino Fig.3a, data/yamashita_kino_lc_T.csv) | 1.3 | +-30% per point | **FAIL** | independent per-point shape check vs the digitized full curve -- 200 K PASS (engine 0.925 vs digitized 0.897, +3.1%); 400 K PASS (engine 1.056 vs digitized 1.113, -5.1%); 600 K PASS (engine 1.180 vs digitized 1.642, -28.2%); 800 K FAIL (engine 1.311 vs digitized 2.358, -44.4%). Registered: the 800 K point is an expected-FAIL (the placeholder T-shape is too flat vs the sourced curve); 200/400/600 K were either-outcome-acceptable pre-run (PRE_REGISTRATION.md amendment 2026-07-13). One test with V_yamashita_lcT / V_yamashita_ratio (counted once below). |
| V_faifman_peak | anchor-consistency | 7.1e9 s^-1 @ 0.423 eV (Fujiwara 2000) | 7099990898.1 | +-25% | PASS | anchor-consistency check: the F=1 peak amplitude IS the inserted measured value (validates the resonance-model construction, not independent physics) |
| V_petitjean_omega | independent | 0.45% measured effective sticking (band [0.40,0.50]; Breunlich/Petitjean) | 0.6 | [0.40,0.50] | **FAIL** | independent prediction of effective sticking from the ledger microphysics (omega_s0 x (1-R_col)); FAIL is the registered finding: the ~+24% gap to the measured 0.45% is the side-channel share the v1 effective parameters absorb (accounting.md re-attribution; pending lambda_ttmu acquisition). |
| V_faifman_900K | independent | 2.3e9 s^-1 (Faifman1989 Maxwellian, 900 K; ledger lambda_dtmu_900K) | 105744440.7 | +-50% | **FAIL** | independent thermal-formation check: statistical hyperfine mix 3/4 F=1 + 1/4 F=0 of the v1 formation model vs the ledger's own Faifman1989 900 K rate; registered expected-FAIL (~20x low) -- the placeholder's thermal scale is anchored near the measured lambda_c(300 K), not the bare Faifman lambda_dtmu (needs_verification carried from rates.csv). |
| V_faifman_lowT | independent | 2e10 s^-1 (Faifman1989 energy-resolved, E=0.2 eV; ledger lambda_dtmu_lowT) | 1162698056.5 | +-50% | **FAIL** | independent energy-resolved check: the v1 resonance-model F=0 branch at E=0.2 eV vs the ledger's own Faifman1989 lambda_dtmu_lowT; registered expected-FAIL (~17x low) -- the placeholder near-threshold F=0 resonances are unsourced geometry, not the bare Faifman rate (needs_verification carried). |
| V_nagamine_trend | independent | solid D-T (5-16 K): cycling rises & losses fall as T falls (RIKEN-RAL) | n/a | qualitative monotone | DEFERRED | DEFERRED: v1 is a thermalized gas/liquid model; solid-phase condensed-matter formation is Phase-3 scope (pre-registered in PRE_REGISTRATION.md) |

**Summary: 6 pass (0 independent), 5 fail (5 registered placeholder-distance findings -- see notes), 1 deferred. Distinct tests: 10 (the three Yamashita rows are one test, disclosed above).**

Gate rule: a target passes only within its pre-registered tolerance; deferred items are documented limitations of the v1 model, not silent passes. No input was tuned to hit a validation target, with one disclosed anchor: the formation model's overall scale (`formation._CALIB`) is set to the room-temperature thermal rate (see formation.py), so yield-level targets at 300 K are not fully independent of that anchor.

The T-shape rows are now split by claim tier: V_yamashita_lcT stays a `shape (calibrated model)` monotonicity check of the calibrated resonance-model construction (hand-placed, unsourced placeholder resonance positions -- see formation.py), NOT a sourced temperature dependence, while V_yamashita_ratio and V_yamashita_curve are `independent` comparators against the digitized Yamashita-Kino 2022 Fig.3a curve (openmucf/data/yamashita_kino_lc_T.csv). The only independent rows are those marked `independent` in the class column; as of this version the passing set contains none -- the FIVE FAILING independent rows are pre-registered findings that measure the v1 placeholder's distance from the field's own rates (PRE_REGISTRATION.md amendments 2026-07-12 / 2026-07-13). The sourced-shape finding: the engine's lambda_c(800 K)/lambda_c(300 K) ~ 1.31 is -41% to -44% below the digitized 2.24-2.36 (outside +-30% across the whole extraction band) -- the first sourced quantification of the placeholder's too-flat temperature shape, and the standing, quantified motivation for the sourced-formation upgrade and the Phase-3 reactivation module.
